other_observers: Joey Kellner and Nick Komar; found by the three of us
description: the bird was a large loon, with the long, low-riding body, thick and fairly short neck, large head, and large, straight bill. It was noticeably larger and a little bulkier, with a thicker neck, than nearby Common Loons that were on the same plane of the reservoir. The back was noticeably browner and paler than those same common loons, and the neck was significantly paler, with a straw brown color quite different from the Common Loons. The bill was very large for a loon, again quite a bit larger than the bills of the Common Loons. It was yellowish in color, and the bird held it above the horizontal for almost the entire observation.Behaviors: the bird was swimming out on the middle of the reservoir and diving occasionally.Call: none heardPlumage: unknown
